free_text: Mass and charge distributions in the very asymmetric
mass region of the neutron induced fission of 238-Np.
Main reference.
Preliminary results.
First author M.Davi.
I.Tsekhanovich,S.Oberstedt, H.R.Faust.
I.Tsekhanovich.
I.Tsekhanovich,H.-O.Denschlag,M.Davi.
Z.Bueyuekmumcu.
F.Goennenwein.
S.Oberstedt.
Recoil mass-separator Lohengrin of
Institute Laue-Langevin (ILL) Grenoble.
Prepared by electrodeposition of NpO2 on a rectangular
surface (5 mm x 40 mm) on a titanium backing.
Thickness of the NpO2-layer was about 110 microg/cm2.
Covered with thin nickel foils (first two targets) or
a tantalum foil (thickness of 0.25 microm and 0.084
microm, respectively).
The isotope undergoing the neutron induced fission
238-Np is formed on line from 237-Np in the high
neutron flux 5.4E+14 /cm**2/s at the target position
of Lohengrin.
1995-1998, three runs.
Fission products from the reaction
237-Np(2nth,f) were detected in an ionization chamber.
The evolution of the target intensity as a function of
exposure time, was measured daily using reference
spectra (A/q = 88/22 at the kinetic energies of 85, 90,
In each mass chain at least 5 ionic charge states
(near the mean kinetic energy) and at least five
different kinetic energies (EL = 85, 90, 95, 100, 105
MeV) (near the mean ionic charge state) were measured.
An exception is the lightest mass (A = 74) where, due
to low count rates, only 4 ionic charge states
(q = 18-21) and only 4 kinetic energy settings (EL =
90, 95, 100, 105) could be measured.
Count rates obtained were corrected for the energy
dispersion of Lohengrin and for "burn-up" of targets.
Mean energy loss for fragments from the targets covered
with nickel was determined as 6.8 MeV (around 90% in
the covering nickel foil and approximately 10% in the
target itself); - in the target covered with tantalum
amounted to 2.3 MeV (70% in the foil and 30% in the
target material). Energy loss was calculated by the
computer code TRIM (Transport of Ions In Matter).
Corrected count rates of the single isotopes (excluding
A=83) at various ionic charge states were fitted to
gaussian distributions.
Experimental values of the ionic charge and widths as
function of atomic mass and nuclear charge were fitted
to the systematics of Nikolaev+ (see REL-REF).
Absolute yields were normalized to the absolute yield
of mass 88 taken from REL-REF of G.Martinez+
M.M.
SD: Ref.C,98SEYSS aded. Correction in
Subent 004.
Normal distributions were used for the description of
the kinetic energy distributions of fission products.
The mean kinetic energies were obtained by the fit of
the count rates of the individual masses at five
different kinetic energies after correction .
Reliable fit of the energy distribution of the isotope
34-Se-83 was not possible; the yield of 83Se was
obtained by subtracting the number of counts observed
for the isotopes with Z = 32,33 and 35 from the number
of counts observed for the total mass (A = 83).
For the energy loss in the targets and covering foils,
Width of kinetic energy distribution.
One standard deviation.
Absolute mass yields .
One standard deviation.
Relative independent yields are given in % in Table,
divided by compiler by 100.
The data for A = 86 and 87 were taken from REL-REF of
G.Martinez+.
The absolute independent yields of the single nuclides
could be obtained by multiplying the fractional
independent yields (Subent 004) by the chain yields
(Subent 003).
One sigma error.
Table 3 of Nucl.Phys.,A688(2001)633.
SD: REACTION code uodated.
Mean nuclear charges.
The data for A = 86 and 87 were taken from REL-REF of
G.Martinez+.
Width of charge distribution.
One sigma error.
Absolute yields of chemical elements..
Total independent yields of the chemical elements were
obtained by absolute isotopic yields fitting to normal
distribution and integration.
One standard deviation.
Number of prompt neutrons.
Number of prompt neutrons was estimated assuming that
an energy of 10 +- 5 MeV is required to boil off one
neutron, and using measured in this work kinetic
energy distributions of fission products.
Increase in mean atomic mass
(deltaAm(Z)/deltaEkin), amu/MeV, with the kinetic
energy of the fragments for the various elements;
derived from a linear fit of the average mass of
various elements.
Not specified.
